title: H001: Implement Keystone Operator packaging
description:
**Size:** 🔨 medium **Category:** infrastructure **Priority:** high ## Open Questions All questions resolved by user decisions. No remaining ambiguities. ## Summary Create the packaging layer for the Keystone operator: a multi-stage Dockerfile (Go 1.25 builder → distroless runtime), a Helm chart with all deployment manifests (Deployment, Service, ServiceAccount, RBAC, webhook configs, CRDs), and a FluxCD HelmRelease to deploy it via GitOps. This connects the existing operator code (`operators/keystone/`) to the existing FluxCD deployment pipeline (`deploy/flux-system/`), completing Phase 8 (S017) of the project plan. ## Scope **Included:** - `operators/keystone/Dockerfile` — multi-stage build (golang:1.25 builder, gcr.io/distroless/static:nonroot runtime, CGO_ENABLED=0, UID 65532). Build context must be workspace root to resolve `go.work` and `internal/common` dependency. - `operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/Chart.yaml` — apiVersion v2, chart metadata - `operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/values.yaml` — image, replicas (default 2), resources, leaderElection (default true), webhook, metrics, serviceAccount - `operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/crds/keystone.openstack.c5c3.io_keystones.yaml` — CRD copied from `config/crd/bases/` - `operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/templates/deployment.yaml` - `operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/templates/service.yaml` - `operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/templates/serviceaccount.yaml` - `operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/templates/clusterrole.yaml` - `operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/templates/clusterrolebinding.yaml` - `operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/templates/webhook-configuration.yaml` (both MutatingWebhookConfiguration and ValidatingWebhookConfiguration) - `operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/templates/_helpers.tpl` - `deploy/flux-system/releases/keystone-operator.yaml` — FluxCD HelmRelease targeting `openstack` namespace - Update `deploy/flux-system/kustomization.yaml` to include the new HelmRelease resource **Excluded:** - CI/CD workflows for building/pushing operator image or Helm chart (S018 — separate feature) - Makefile `docker-build` and `helm-package` target implementations (separate feature per PLAN.md) - `values-prod.yaml` or environment-specific overrides (YAGNI) - `NOTES.txt` post-install template (YAGNI) - Helm chart tests (YAGNI for initial packaging) - HelmRepository source for c5c3-charts (`deploy/flux-system/sources/c5c3-charts.yaml` already exists) - Monitoring/alerting resources — PrometheusRule, ServiceMonitor (separate concern) - New namespace manifest (`openstack` already exists in `namespaces.yaml`) ## Visualization ```mermaid flowchart TD subgraph build ["Dockerfile - Multi-stage Build"] B1["golang:1.25 builder"] B2["CGO_ENABLED=0 go build"] B3["distroless/static:nonroot"] B1 --> B2 --> B3 end subgraph helm ["Helm Chart - keystone-operator"] CH["Chart.yaml"] VA["values.yaml"] CRD["crds/keystones.yaml"] subgraph templates ["templates/"] DEP["deployment.yaml"] SVC["service.yaml"] SA["serviceaccount.yaml"] CR["clusterrole.yaml"] CRB["clusterrolebinding.yaml"] MWH["mutating-webhook-config"] VWH["validating-webhook-config"] HLP["_helpers.tpl"] end end subgraph flux ["FluxCD GitOps"] SRC["HelmRepository: c5c3-charts
oci://ghcr.io/c5c3/charts"] HR["HelmRelease: keystone-operator"] SRC --> HR end B3 -->|"image referenced by"| VA VA -->|"configures"| templates HR -->|"deploys chart"| helm ``` ```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant FC as FluxCD participant HR as HelmRelease participant H as Helm participant K as Kubernetes participant CM as cert-manager FC->>HR: Reconcile keystone-operator Note over HR: dependsOn: cert-manager,
mariadb-operator, memcached-operator HR->>H: Install/Upgrade chart H->>K: Apply CRDs from crds/ H->>K: Create ServiceAccount H->>K: Create ClusterRole + ClusterRoleBinding H->>K: Create Deployment (2 replicas) H->>K: Create Service (webhook 443 to 9443, metrics 8080) H->>K: Create MutatingWebhookConfiguration H->>K: Create ValidatingWebhookConfiguration K->>CM: cert-manager.io/inject-ca-from annotation CM-->>K: Inject CA bundle into webhook configs K-->>H: Resources ready H-->>HR: Release healthy HR-->>FC: Ready=True ``` ```mermaid flowchart LR subgraph deps ["HelmRelease Dependency Chain"] CM["cert-manager"] MDB["mariadb-operator"] MC["memcached-operator"] ES["external-secrets"] KS["keystone-operator"] CM --> MDB CM --> MC CM --> ES MDB --> KS MC --> KS CM --> KS end ``` ## Key Components - **`operators/keystone/Dockerfile`**: Two-stage build. Builder stage uses `golang:1.25`, copies `go.work`, `go.sum`, and all three module directories (`internal/common/`, `operators/keystone/`, `operators/c5c3/`) for workspace-aware dependency resolution. Builds with `CGO_ENABLED=0 GOOS=linux go build -a -o manager ./operators/keystone/main.go`. Runtime stage copies binary to `gcr.io/distroless/static:nonroot`, runs as UID 65532:65532. Docker build context is workspace root (`/workspace`), not `operators/keystone/`. - **`Chart.yaml`**: Chart name `keystone-operator`, apiVersion `v2`, type `application`, version `0.1.0`, appVersion `0.1.0`. - **`values.yaml`**: Configurable image (repo/tag/pullPolicy), replicas (default 2), resource requests/limits, leaderElection toggle (default true), webhook enable/disable, metrics port (default 8080 — matches bootstrap default `--metrics-bind-address :8080`), serviceAccount name/create flag. - **`crds/keystone.openstack.c5c3.io_keystones.yaml`**: Copy of generated CRD from `config/crd/bases/`. Helm auto-installs CRDs from `crds/` directory before templates. - **`templates/deployment.yaml`**: Operator Deployment using `bootstrap.Run()` flags: `--leader-elect={{ .Values.leaderElection.enabled }}`, `--metrics-bind-address=:{{ .Values.metrics.port }}`, `--health-probe-bind-address=:8081`. Liveness/readiness probes on `:8081` (`/healthz`, `/readyz`). Security context: non-root, read-only rootfs, drop ALL capabilities, `allowPrivilegeEscalation: false`, `runAsNonRoot: true`. Resource limits from values. Webhook port 9443 exposed on container. - **`templates/service.yaml`**: ClusterIP service exposing webhook port (443→9443) and metrics port (8080). - **`templates/serviceaccount.yaml`**: Conditionally created SA via `{{ if .Values.serviceAccount.create }}`. - **`templates/clusterrole.yaml`**: RBAC rules derived from `+kubebuilder:rbac` markers in `keystone_controller.go`: - `keystone.openstack.c5c3.io`: keystones, keystones/status, keystones/finalizers - `apps`: deployments - `""` (core): services, configmaps, secrets, serviceaccounts, events - `batch`: jobs, cronjobs - `k8s.mariadb.com`: databases, users, grants - `external-secrets.io`: externalsecrets, pushsecrets - `rbac.authorization.k8s.io`: roles, rolebindings - **`templates/clusterrolebinding.yaml`**: Binds ClusterRole to the operator's ServiceAccount in the `openstack` namespace. - **`templates/webhook-configuration.yaml`**: Both `MutatingWebhookConfiguration` (defaulting, path `/mutate-keystone-openstack-c5c3-io-v1alpha1-keystone`, operations: CREATE+UPDATE, name: `mkeystone.kb.io`) and `ValidatingWebhookConfiguration` (validation, path `/validate-keystone-openstack-c5c3-io-v1alpha1-keystone`, operations: CREATE+UPDATE+DELETE, name: `vkeystone.kb.io`). Both with `failurePolicy: Fail`, `sideEffects: None`, and `cert-manager.io/inject-ca-from` annotation for automatic CA bundle injection. - **`templates/_helpers.tpl`**: Standard helpers — `fullname`, `name`, `chart`, `labels`, `selectorLabels`, `serviceAccountName`. - **`deploy/flux-system/releases/keystone-operator.yaml`**: HelmRelease (apiVersion `helm.toolkit.fluxcd.io/v2`) targeting `openstack` namespace. References `c5c3-charts` OCI HelmRepository (namespace `flux-system`). `dependsOn: [cert-manager, mariadb-operator, memcached-operator]` with correct namespace references. `install.crds: CreateReplace`, `upgrade.crds: CreateReplace`, `remediation.retries: 3`. Version range `>=0.1.0 <1.0.0`. Interval `30m`. Values override: `replicas: 2`, `leaderElection.enabled: true`. - **`deploy/flux-system/kustomization.yaml`**: Add `releases/keystone-operator.yaml` to the resources list (alongside existing releases).

stories.title: Release engineer builds operator container image
stories.want: to build a minimal, secure container image for the Keystone operator using a multi-stage Dockerfile
stories.so_that: the operator can be deployed as a container in Kubernetes with minimal attack surface
criteria: Dockerfile uses golang:1.25 builder stage with CGO_ENABLED=0 and GOOS=linux
Dockerfile uses gcr.io/distroless/static:nonroot as runtime stage
Build context is workspace root (/workspace) to resolve go.work and internal/common dependency
Runtime image runs as UID 65532:65532 (nonroot)
Binary is compiled from operators/keystone/main.go and placed at /manager in runtime stage

stories.title: Platform engineer deploys keystone-operator via FluxCD
stories.want: to deploy the Keystone operator through the existing FluxCD GitOps pipeline using a HelmRelease
stories.so_that: the operator is deployed consistently and automatically via GitOps with correct dependency ordering
criteria: HelmRelease references c5c3-charts OCI HelmRepository in flux-system namespace
HelmRelease targets openstack namespace
HelmRelease dependsOn cert-manager, mariadb-operator, and memcached-operator with correct namespace references
HelmRelease uses install/upgrade crds: CreateReplace with remediation retries: 3
kustomization.yaml includes releases/keystone-operator.yaml in the resources list

stories.title: Platform engineer customizes operator deployment via Helm values
stories.want: to configure operator replicas, resources, leader election, metrics, and webhook settings through Helm values
stories.so_that: I can tune the operator deployment for different environments without modifying templates
criteria: values.yaml exposes image.repository, image.tag, image.pullPolicy with sensible defaults
values.yaml exposes replicas (default 2), resources with requests/limits
values.yaml exposes leaderElection.enabled (default true), metrics.port (default 8080)
values.yaml exposes webhook.enabled (default true) and serviceAccount.create/name
All template parameters are correctly referenced in deployment, service, and webhook templates

stories.title: Cluster admin verifies operator security posture
stories.want: the operator deployment to follow least-privilege security principles
stories.so_that: the operator does not pose unnecessary security risks to the cluster
criteria: Deployment securityContext sets runAsNonRoot: true, readOnlyRootFilesystem: true
Container securityContext sets allowPrivilegeEscalation: false and drops ALL capabilities
ClusterRole includes only permissions defined by kubebuilder RBAC markers in keystone_controller.go
ClusterRoleBinding binds to the operator ServiceAccount in the release namespace only
Container runs as seccompProfile type RuntimeDefault

stories.title: Platform engineer manages webhooks with cert-manager integration
stories.want: MutatingWebhookConfiguration and ValidatingWebhookConfiguration to be deployed with automatic CA bundle injection from cert-manager
stories.so_that: webhook TLS is managed automatically without manual certificate provisioning
criteria: MutatingWebhookConfiguration has path /mutate-keystone-openstack-c5c3-io-v1alpha1-keystone for CREATE and UPDATE
ValidatingWebhookConfiguration has path /validate-keystone-openstack-c5c3-io-v1alpha1-keystone for CREATE, UPDATE, and DELETE
Both configurations include cert-manager.io/inject-ca-from annotation referencing the release namespace certificate
Both configurations set failurePolicy: Fail and sideEffects: None
Webhook configurations are conditionally rendered based on webhook.enabled value

stories.title: CRDs are automatically installed with Helm chart
stories.want: the Keystone CRD to be automatically installed and upgraded when the Helm chart is deployed
stories.so_that: CRD lifecycle is managed alongside the operator without separate manual steps
criteria: CRD file keystone.openstack.c5c3.io_keystones.yaml exists in helm chart crds/ directory
CRD content matches the generated CRD in config/crd/bases/
FluxCD HelmRelease uses crds: CreateReplace for both install and upgrade
Helm automatically installs CRDs from crds/ directory before rendering templates

requirements.description: The Dockerfile SHALL produce a minimal container image using multi-stage build with distroless runtime and non-root user
requirements.rationale: Minimal images reduce attack surface, non-root execution prevents privilege escalation, multi-stage builds keep image size small

scenarios.name: Multi-stage build produces minimal image
scenarios.when: docker build is executed with workspace root as build context
scenarios.then: the builder stage compiles the Go binary with CGO_ENABLED=0 GOOS=linux
and_then: the runtime stage uses gcr.io/distroless/static:nonroot
only the compiled binary is present in the final image

scenarios.name: Image runs as non-root user
scenarios.when: the container starts
scenarios.then: the process runs as UID 65532 (nonroot) and GID 65532
and_then: USER directive is set to 65532:65532 in the Dockerfile

scenarios.name: Build fails if Go source has compilation errors
scenarios.when: docker build is executed and Go source code has compilation errors
scenarios.then: the build fails at the go build step with clear error output
and_then: no image is produced

requirements.description: The Dockerfile SHALL use workspace root as build context to resolve go.work and internal/common module dependencies
requirements.rationale: The operator module depends on internal/common via go.work workspace replace directive; build context must include all workspace modules

scenarios.name: Go workspace modules are resolved
scenarios.when: docker build runs with workspace root context
scenarios.then: go.work, go.sum, and all module directories (internal/common/, operators/keystone/, operators/c5c3/) are available in builder
and_then: go build resolves the replace directive for internal/common

scenarios.name: Build fails with wrong context
scenarios.when: docker build runs with operators/keystone/ as context instead of workspace root
scenarios.then: the build fails because go.work and internal/common/ are not available
and_then: error indicates missing module dependency

requirements.description: The Helm chart SHALL include all required Kubernetes resource templates: Deployment, Service, ServiceAccount, ClusterRole, ClusterRoleBinding, MutatingWebhookConfiguration, ValidatingWebhookConfiguration, and CRD
requirements.rationale: A complete chart ensures the operator is fully functional after installation with all necessary RBAC, networking, and admission control

scenarios.name: All templates render successfully
scenarios.when: helm template is executed with default values
scenarios.then: all template files render valid YAML without errors
and_then: output contains Deployment, Service, ServiceAccount, ClusterRole, ClusterRoleBinding, MutatingWebhookConfiguration, ValidatingWebhookConfiguration

scenarios.name: Chart passes helm lint
scenarios.when: helm lint is executed on the chart directory
scenarios.then: no errors are reported
and_then: chart metadata (Chart.yaml) is valid with apiVersion v2, type application

scenarios.name: Standard labels are applied to all resources
scenarios.when: helm template renders any resource
scenarios.then: each resource has helm.sh/chart, app.kubernetes.io/name, app.kubernetes.io/instance, app.kubernetes.io/version, and app.kubernetes.io/managed-by labels

requirements.description: The Helm chart values SHALL expose all operational parameters including image, replicas, resources, leaderElection, webhook, metrics, and serviceAccount
requirements.rationale: Configurable values enable environment-specific tuning without template modification, following Helm best practices

scenarios.name: Default values produce valid deployment
scenarios.when: helm template is executed without value overrides
scenarios.then: all templates render with sensible defaults (replicas: 2, leaderElection: true, metrics port: 8080)
and_then: deployment is functional with default resource limits

scenarios.name: Custom values override defaults
scenarios.when: helm template is executed with --set replicas=3,resources.limits.memory=256Mi
scenarios.then: the rendered Deployment has 3 replicas and 256Mi memory limit
and_then: all other values retain their defaults

scenarios.name: Webhook can be disabled
scenarios.when: helm template is executed with --set webhook.enabled=false
scenarios.then: MutatingWebhookConfiguration and ValidatingWebhookConfiguration are not rendered
and_then: Deployment, Service, and RBAC resources still render correctly

requirements.description: The ClusterRole SHALL include exactly the RBAC permissions defined by kubebuilder RBAC markers in keystone_controller.go
requirements.rationale: RBAC rules must match the operator's actual API access requirements — too few causes runtime errors, too many violates least-privilege

scenarios.name: ClusterRole includes all required API group permissions
scenarios.when: helm template renders the ClusterRole
scenarios.then: rules include: keystone.openstack.c5c3.io (keystones, keystones/status, keystones/finalizers), apps (deployments), core (services, configmaps, secrets, serviceaccounts, events), batch (jobs, cronjobs), k8s.mariadb.com (databases, users, grants), external-secrets.io (externalsecrets, pushsecrets), rbac.authorization.k8s.io (roles, rolebindings)
and_then: no additional API groups or resources are granted beyond what kubebuilder markers specify

scenarios.name: Verb permissions match kubebuilder markers
scenarios.when: helm template renders the ClusterRole
scenarios.then: each rule has exactly the verbs specified in the corresponding kubebuilder RBAC marker
and_then: events only have create and patch verbs
external-secrets resources do not have delete verb

requirements.description: The Deployment SHALL configure pod and container security context following Kubernetes security best practices
requirements.rationale: Security context prevents privilege escalation, limits filesystem access, and ensures containers run with minimal privileges

scenarios.name: Container security context enforces least privilege
scenarios.when: helm template renders the Deployment
scenarios.then: container securityContext includes: allowPrivilegeEscalation: false, capabilities.drop: [ALL], readOnlyRootFilesystem: true, runAsNonRoot: true
and_then: seccompProfile.type is RuntimeDefault

scenarios.name: Pod runs as non-root user
scenarios.when: helm template renders the Deployment
scenarios.then: pod securityContext includes: runAsNonRoot: true, runAsUser: 65532, runAsGroup: 65532, fsGroup: 65532

requirements.description: The webhook configurations SHALL include cert-manager.io/inject-ca-from annotation for automatic CA bundle injection
requirements.rationale: cert-manager manages TLS certificates for webhooks; the inject-ca-from annotation enables automatic CA bundle injection into webhook configurations

scenarios.name: Mutating webhook has cert-manager annotation
scenarios.when: helm template renders the MutatingWebhookConfiguration
scenarios.then: metadata.annotations includes cert-manager.io/inject-ca-from pointing to the release namespace and certificate name
and_then: webhook clientConfig.service references the operator service name and webhook port

scenarios.name: Validating webhook has cert-manager annotation
scenarios.when: helm template renders the ValidatingWebhookConfiguration
scenarios.then: metadata.annotations includes cert-manager.io/inject-ca-from pointing to the release namespace and certificate name
and_then: webhook has failurePolicy: Fail and sideEffects: None

scenarios.name: Webhook paths match kubebuilder markers
scenarios.when: helm template renders both webhook configurations
scenarios.then: mutating webhook path is /mutate-keystone-openstack-c5c3-io-v1alpha1-keystone and validating webhook path is /validate-keystone-openstack-c5c3-io-v1alpha1-keystone
and_then: mutating webhook operations are CREATE and UPDATE
validating webhook operations are CREATE, UPDATE, and DELETE

requirements.description: The FluxCD HelmRelease SHALL follow existing deployment patterns with correct dependency chain (dependsOn cert-manager, mariadb-operator, memcached-operator)
requirements.rationale: Dependency ordering ensures prerequisite operators (cert-manager for TLS, mariadb-operator and memcached-operator for CRDs) are ready before keystone-operator starts

scenarios.name: HelmRelease follows memcached-operator pattern
scenarios.when: the HelmRelease YAML is created
scenarios.then: it uses apiVersion helm.toolkit.fluxcd.io/v2, interval 30m, references c5c3-charts HelmRepository, and uses crds: CreateReplace for install/upgrade
and_then: remediation retries is 3 for both install and upgrade

scenarios.name: Dependency chain is correct
scenarios.when: FluxCD reconciles the HelmRelease
scenarios.then: dependsOn includes cert-manager (cert-manager namespace), mariadb-operator (mariadb-system namespace), and memcached-operator (memcached-system namespace)
and_then: keystone-operator is not reconciled until all dependencies report Ready

scenarios.name: HelmRelease is registered in kustomization
scenarios.when: FluxCD processes the kustomization.yaml
scenarios.then: releases/keystone-operator.yaml is included in the resources list
and_then: resource appears after existing release entries in logical ordering

requirements.description: The Helm chart SHALL include the Keystone CRD in the crds/ directory matching the generated CRD from config/crd/bases/
requirements.rationale: Helm automatically installs CRDs from the crds/ directory before templates, ensuring the CRD exists before the operator starts reconciling

scenarios.name: CRD is present in chart crds/ directory
scenarios.when: the Helm chart is packaged or lint is run
scenarios.then: crds/keystone.openstack.c5c3.io_keystones.yaml exists and is valid YAML
and_then: CRD defines the Keystone kind in group keystone.openstack.c5c3.io

scenarios.name: CRD content matches source
scenarios.when: the CRD in crds/ is compared to config/crd/bases/keystone.openstack.c5c3.io_keystones.yaml
scenarios.then: the files are identical
and_then: no manual modifications have been applied to the chart copy

requirements.description: The Deployment SHALL configure health probes matching the bootstrap.Run() default bind addresses
requirements.rationale: Health probes enable Kubernetes to detect unhealthy operator instances for automatic restart and readiness gating

scenarios.name: Liveness probe targets healthz endpoint
scenarios.when: helm template renders the Deployment
scenarios.then: livenessProbe uses httpGet on path /healthz port 8081
and_then: initialDelaySeconds and periodSeconds are configured

scenarios.name: Readiness probe targets readyz endpoint
scenarios.when: helm template renders the Deployment
scenarios.then: readinessProbe uses httpGet on path /readyz port 8081
and_then: initialDelaySeconds and periodSeconds are configured

scenarios.name: Container args match bootstrap.Run() flags
scenarios.when: helm template renders the Deployment container
scenarios.then: args include --leader-elect, --metrics-bind-address, and --health-probe-bind-address flags
and_then: metrics-bind-address uses the configurable metrics.port from values
health-probe-bind-address is hardcoded to :8081

tasks.title: Create operators/keystone/Dockerfile with multi-stage build: golang:1.25 builder with CGO_ENABLED=0, workspace root context copying go.work + all modules, gcr.io/distroless/static:nonroot runtime with USER 65532:65532 (CC-0017, REQ-001, REQ-002)

Create Chart.yaml (apiVersion v2, name keystone-operator, version 0.1.0, appVersion 0.1.0, type application) and values.yaml with all configurable parameters: image (repo/tag/pullPolicy), replicas (default 2), resources (requests/limits), leaderElection.enabled (default true), webhook.enabled (default true), metrics.port (default 8080), serviceAccount.create/name (CC-0017, REQ-003, REQ-004)

Create _helpers.tpl with standard Helm template helpers: keystone-operator.name, keystone-operator.fullname, keystone-operator.chart, keystone-operator.labels (helm.sh/chart, app.kubernetes.io/name, instance, version, managed-by), keystone-operator.selectorLabels, keystone-operator.serviceAccountName (CC-0017, REQ-003)

Copy CRD from operators/keystone/config/crd/bases/keystone.openstack.c5c3.io_keystones.yaml to operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/crds/keystone.openstack.c5c3.io_keystones.yaml — exact copy with no modifications (CC-0017, REQ-009)

Create RBAC templates: serviceaccount.yaml (conditional on serviceAccount.create), clusterrole.yaml with all rules from kubebuilder RBAC markers in keystone_controller.go (7 API groups with exact verbs), clusterrolebinding.yaml binding ClusterRole to ServiceAccount in release namespace (CC-0017, REQ-003, REQ-005)

Create deployment.yaml: operator Deployment with container args (--leader-elect, --metrics-bind-address, --health-probe-bind-address), liveness probe (/healthz:8081), readiness probe (/readyz:8081), webhook container port 9443, pod securityContext (runAsNonRoot, UID/GID 65532, fsGroup), container securityContext (drop ALL, read-only rootfs, no privilege escalation, seccomp RuntimeDefault), resource limits from values (CC-0017, REQ-003, REQ-006, REQ-010)
Create service.yaml: ClusterIP Service exposing webhook port (443 targetPort 9443, name webhook) and metrics port (8080, name metrics), using selectorLabels from _helpers.tpl (CC-0017, REQ-003)

Create webhook-configuration.yaml: MutatingWebhookConfiguration (name mkeystone.kb.io, path /mutate-keystone-openstack-c5c3-io-v1alpha1-keystone, operations CREATE+UPDATE) and ValidatingWebhookConfiguration (name vkeystone.kb.io, path /validate-keystone-openstack-c5c3-io-v1alpha1-keystone, operations CREATE+UPDATE+DELETE), both with cert-manager.io/inject-ca-from annotation, failurePolicy Fail, sideEffects None, conditional on webhook.enabled (CC-0017, REQ-003, REQ-007)

Create deploy/flux-system/releases/keystone-operator.yaml HelmRelease (helm.toolkit.fluxcd.io/v2, namespace openstack, interval 30m, sourceRef c5c3-charts, version >=0.1.0 <1.0.0, dependsOn cert-manager + mariadb-operator + memcached-operator with namespaces, crds CreateReplace, retries 3, values: replicas 2, leaderElection.enabled true) and update deploy/flux-system/kustomization.yaml to include releases/keystone-operator.yaml in resources list (CC-0017, REQ-008)

Validate complete Helm chart: run helm lint, helm template with default values, helm template with webhook.enabled=false, helm template with custom value overrides. Verify all rendered YAML is valid, all resources have correct labels, security contexts, RBAC rules match kubebuilder markers, probe endpoints match bootstrap.Run() defaults, webhook paths match config/webhook/manifests.yaml (CC-0017, REQ-001 through REQ-010)

Create reference documentation at docs/reference/backend/keystone-operator-packaging.md covering: Dockerfile build instructions (build context, build args, image layers), Helm chart configuration reference (all values.yaml parameters with types, defaults, and descriptions), FluxCD HelmRelease configuration, dependency chain diagram, and CRD installation behavior (CC-0017)

test_specifications.story: Release engineer builds operator container image
test_specifications.expected: Dockerfile passes hadolint checks with no errors — correct FROM directives, USER 65532:65532, no unnecessary layers

test_specifications.story: Release engineer builds operator container image
test_specifications.expected: COPY directives reference go.work, go.sum, internal/common/, operators/keystone/, and operators/c5c3/ relative to workspace root

test_specifications.story: Platform engineer deploys keystone-operator via FluxCD
test_specifications.expected: helm lint operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/ exits 0 with no errors

test_specifications.story: Platform engineer deploys keystone-operator via FluxCD
test_specifications.expected: helm template renders Deployment, Service, ServiceAccount, ClusterRole, ClusterRoleBinding, MutatingWebhookConfiguration, and ValidatingWebhookConfiguration

test_specifications.story: Platform engineer deploys keystone-operator via FluxCD
test_specifications.expected: All rendered resources include standard Helm labels (helm.sh/chart, app.kubernetes.io/name, app.kubernetes.io/instance, app.kubernetes.io/managed-by)

test_specifications.story: Platform engineer customizes operator deployment via Helm values
test_specifications.expected: Default values produce Deployment with 2 replicas, leader-elect=true, metrics port 8080

test_specifications.story: Platform engineer customizes operator deployment via Helm values
test_specifications.expected: Custom --set values (replicas=3, resources.limits.memory=256Mi) are reflected in rendered Deployment

test_specifications.story: Platform engineer customizes operator deployment via Helm values
test_specifications.expected: Setting webhook.enabled=false omits MutatingWebhookConfiguration and ValidatingWebhookConfiguration from output

test_specifications.story: Cluster admin verifies operator security posture
test_specifications.expected: ClusterRole rules include all 7 API groups from kubebuilder RBAC markers: keystone.openstack.c5c3.io, apps, core, batch, k8s.mariadb.com, external-secrets.io, rbac.authorization.k8s.io

test_specifications.story: Cluster admin verifies operator security posture
test_specifications.expected: Events rule has only create;patch verbs, external-secrets rule has get;list;watch;create;update;patch (no delete), other CRUD rules have full verb set

test_specifications.story: Cluster admin verifies operator security posture
test_specifications.expected: Container securityContext has allowPrivilegeEscalation: false, capabilities.drop: [ALL], readOnlyRootFilesystem: true, runAsNonRoot: true, seccompProfile.type: RuntimeDefault

review_criteria: Dockerfile builds successfully with workspace root context: CGO_ENABLED=0, golang:1.25 builder, distroless/static:nonroot runtime, USER 65532:65532

helm lint passes with no errors and helm template renders all 7 resource types (Deployment, Service, ServiceAccount, ClusterRole, ClusterRoleBinding, MutatingWebhookConfiguration, ValidatingWebhookConfiguration)
ClusterRole RBAC rules exactly match kubebuilder RBAC markers in keystone_controller.go — no extra permissions, no missing permissions
Deployment security context follows least-privilege: runAsNonRoot, drop ALL capabilities, readOnlyRootFilesystem, seccomp RuntimeDefault, no privilege escalation

Webhook configurations have correct paths (/mutate-... and /validate-...), operations (CREATE+UPDATE and CREATE+UPDATE+DELETE), failurePolicy Fail, sideEffects None, and cert-manager.io/inject-ca-from annotation
FluxCD HelmRelease follows memcached-operator pattern: c5c3-charts sourceRef, version range, dependsOn with correct namespaces, crds CreateReplace, remediation retries 3
CRD in crds/ directory is identical to config/crd/bases/keystone.openstack.c5c3.io_keystones.yaml
Container args correctly reference bootstrap.Run() CLI flags: --leader-elect, --metrics-bind-address (configurable via values), --health-probe-bind-address=:8081
All configurable values work: replicas, resources, leaderElection.enabled, webhook.enabled, metrics.port, serviceAccount.create/name, image.repository/tag/pullPolicy
kustomization.yaml updated with releases/keystone-operator.yaml entry in correct position
implementation_notes:
Architecture: This is a pure infrastructure packaging feature — no application code changes. All files are declarative YAML/Dockerfile with no business logic.

Dockerfile Strategy: Multi-stage build with workspace root context. The Go workspace (go.work) with replace directives requires all modules (internal/common/, operators/keystone/, operators/c5c3/) to be available at build time. Build stage copies go.work, go.sum files first for layer caching, then copies source directories. Runtime stage is distroless/static:nonroot for minimal attack surface.

Helm Chart Design: Standard kubebuilder-style operator chart. values.yaml exposes operational knobs without exposing template complexity. _helpers.tpl follows Helm best practices with standard label/selector helpers. CRD is in crds/ directory (not templates/) so Helm installs it before templates, avoiding chicken-and-egg issues.

RBAC Derivation: ClusterRole rules are manually derived from kubebuilder RBAC markers in keystone_controller.go. This is a one-time manual step since we don't have make manifests generating the Helm chart. Future CI should validate RBAC rules match markers.

Webhook Configuration: Both MutatingWebhookConfiguration and ValidatingWebhookConfiguration reference the operator Service for webhook callbacks. cert-manager.io/inject-ca-from annotation enables automatic CA bundle injection from cert-manager Certificate resource. Webhook port is 9443 (container) mapped to 443 (Service).

FluxCD Integration: HelmRelease follows the established pattern (memcached-operator.yaml as reference). dependsOn ensures cert-manager (for TLS), mariadb-operator (for Database/User/Grant CRDs), and memcached-operator (for Memcached CRDs) are ready before keystone-operator starts.

Key Pitfalls:
1. Build context must be workspace root, not operators/keystone/ — the go.work replace directive breaks otherwise
2. RBAC rules must exactly match kubebuilder markers — events only gets create;patch, external-secrets resources don't get delete
3. Webhook service name in configurations must match the Service created by the chart
4. Health probe port 8081 is hardcoded in bootstrap.Run() defaults — not configurable via values
5. CRD copy must be exact — no manual modifications that would diverge from generated source

Data Flow:
```
FluxCD → reconciles HelmRelease → Helm installs CRDs (crds/) → Helm renders templates → K8s creates resources → cert-manager injects CA → operator starts → reconciles Keystone CRs
```

Component Relationships:
```
Dockerfile ──builds──> container image
                           │
values.yaml ──configures──> templates/ ──renders──> K8s resources
                           │
Chart.yaml ──metadata──> Helm chart
                           │
FluxCD HelmRelease ──deploys──> Helm chart ──into──> openstack namespace
```
